title: Tabularis Plugin Manifest
description: Schema for the manifest.json file required by every Tabularis database driver plugin.

id.description: Unique driver identifier (lowercase letters, digits, hyphens). Must match the plugin folder name.

name.description: Display name shown in the UI (e.g. "DuckDB").

description.description: Short description shown in the plugins list.

default_port.description: Default TCP port for the database. Use null for file-based databases.

is_builtin.description: true for built-in drivers shipped with Tabularis. External plugins should omit this field or set it to false.

default_username.description: Default username pre-filled in the connection modal (e.g. "postgres", "root"). Leave empty if there is no conventional default.

executable.description: Relative path to the plugin executable inside the plugin folder (without extension on Linux/macOS; Tabularis appends .exe on Windows automatically).

capabilities.description: Feature flags that control which UI elements Tabularis renders for this driver.

schemas.description: true if the database supports named schemas (e.g. PostgreSQL). Shows the schema selector in the connection UI.

views.description: true to enable the Views section in the database explorer.

routines.description: true to enable stored procedures and functions in the database explorer.

triggers.description: true if the driver supports trigger metadata and trigger management RPCs.

file_based.description: true for local file databases (e.g. SQLite, DuckDB). Replaces the host/port fields with a file path input.

folder_based.description: true for plugins that connect to a directory rather than a single file (e.g. CSV plugin). Replaces host/port fields with a folder picker.

connection_string.description: Optional capability for network drivers. Set false to hide connection string import in the UI.

connectionString.description: CamelCase alias of connection_string. Optional capability for network drivers.

connection_string_example.description: Optional placeholder example shown in the connection string field (e.g. "postgres://user:pass@localhost:5432/db").

identifier_quote.description: Character used to quote SQL identifiers: double-quote for ANSI SQL, backtick for MySQL-style.

sql_dialect.description: SQL dialect used by Tabularis for statement splitting and query classification. Oracle-like plugins can use oracle.

alter_primary_key.description: true if the database supports altering primary keys after table creation.

auto_increment_keyword.description: Keyword appended after the column type for auto-increment columns (e.g. "AUTO_INCREMENT" for MySQL). Empty string if not applicable.

serial_type.description: Replacement data type used for auto-increment columns (e.g. "SERIAL" for PostgreSQL). Empty string if not applicable.

inline_pk.description: true if primary key is defined inline in the column definition (e.g. SQLite AUTOINCREMENT style).

alter_column.description: true if the driver supports ALTER TABLE MODIFY/ALTER COLUMN on existing tables.

create_foreign_keys.description: true if the driver properly supports creating foreign key constraints.

no_connection_required.description: true for API-based plugins that need no host/port/credentials (e.g. public REST APIs). Hides the entire connection form and skips database validation.

manage_tables.description: true if the driver supports table and column management (CREATE TABLE, ADD/MODIFY/DROP COLUMN, DROP TABLE). Does not control index or foreign key operations. Defaults to true.

readonly.description: When true, the driver is read-only: all data modification operations (INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE) are disabled in the UI. Table and column management is also hidden regardless of manage_tables. Defaults to false.

supports_ssl.description: true to show the SSL/TLS configuration tab (mode + CA/client cert/key) in the connection modal for this network driver. Defaults to false.

interpreter.description: Optional interpreter for script-based plugins (e.g. python3).

settings.description: Optional list of configuration fields the plugin exposes to the user.

data_types.description: List of SQL data types supported by this driver for column creation in the UI.

name.description: SQL type name as it appears in DDL (e.g. "VARCHAR", "BIGINT").

category.description: UI grouping category for this type.

requires_length.description: true if this type requires a length argument (e.g. VARCHAR(255)).

requires_precision.description: true if this type requires a precision/scale argument (e.g. DECIMAL(10,2)).

default_length.description: Optional default length value pre-filled in the UI (e.g. "255" for VARCHAR).
